basics.description: **Note:** Please refer to the following before installing the solution: â€˘ Review the solution [Release Notes](https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel/tree/master/Solutions/StealthTalk/ReleaseNotes.md) â€˘ There may be [known issues](https://aka.ms/sentinelsolutionsknownissues) pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing. The **StealthTalk Anomalous Authentication** solution turns StealthTalk Enterprise â€” the patented SDNP-based private business messenger â€” into a first-class data source for Microsoft Sentinel. Customers running StealthTalk on their own Microsoft Azure infrastructure can now operate StealthTalk anomalies through standard Sentinel workflows: SOC analysts triage StealthTalk incidents the same way they triage Azure AD or Defender ones. **What's inside** - **Data Connector.** Custom Logs ingestion via the Logs Ingestion API. Includes a custom log table (parameterised name, default `StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL`), Data Collection Endpoint and Data Collection Rule with a 21-field stream. Step-by-step setup is published inside the connector definition. - **4 scheduled Analytic Rules** with full MITRE ATT&CK mapping and entity mappings (Account / Host / IP): - *After Hours Work* (Low) â€” systematic off-hours logins across â‰Ą 2 distinct days. - *Multi New Devices Registration* (Medium) â€” â‰Ą 2 new devices enrolled by the same user inside 24 h. - *Login Outside Work Zone* (High) â€” login from a country/city different from the user's assigned zone. - *Password Brute Force* (High) â€” â‰Ą 9 consecutive failed authentications. - **3 Hunting Queries** for proactive SOC investigations: Impossible Travel, Account Takeover Sequence (Off-Hours + New Device + Geo), Brute Force Followed By Suspicious Access. - **ASIM Authentication 0.1.3 parsers.** A filtering `vimAuthenticationStealthTalk` and a non-filtering `ASimAuthenticationStealthTalk` follow the schema contract, plus an `imAuthentication` extension parser that registers the StealthTalk source in the workspace ASIM union â€” letting any ASIM-aware detection or hunting query downstream automatically recognise StealthTalk events. - **Interactive Workbook (17 panels)** across Overview, Off-Hours, New Devices, Geo Anomaly, and Brute Force sections. Includes a composite **User Risk Leaderboard** (High=10 / Medium=5 / Low=1 per event), a **Multi-Vector Correlation** view that flags users triggering â‰Ą 2 anomaly types simultaneously, an interactive **World Map**, and three global filters (Time Range, Severity, User ID). - **Microsoft Teams Playbook.** Logic App that posts a formatted Adaptive Card with each StealthTalk incident summary into a Teams channel via a Workflows incoming webhook. Webhook URL is a deployment parameter. **Why partner with StealthTalk.** StealthTalk implements the patented **SDNP** protocol originally developed for emergency-response services in the US, Europe and the Middle East. It bridges the gap between consumer messaging apps and professional security solutions, giving organisations call/IM privacy with full enterprise control. StealthTalk is a Microsoft Solutions Partner with Specializations and ISV Success status, and StealthTalk is **IP Co-sell Eligible** with **Certified software for Security**. **Pre-requisites.** A Sentinel-enabled Log Analytics workspace, a deployed StealthTalk Enterprise instance, an Entra App Registration granted **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** on the deployed DCR, and (for the Teams playbook) a Teams Workflows incoming webhook. Microsoft's `FullDeploymentAuthentication.json` ASIM parser pack must be deployed in the workspace before the Solution is installed â€” the Solution adds the StealthTalk source to the existing union via an extension parser. Detailed installation, validation and rollback instructions are published with the Solution. Support: support@stealthtalk.com. **Data Connectors:** 1, **Workbooks:** 1, **Analytic Rules:** 4, **Hunting Queries:** 3, **Playbooks:** 1 [Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel](https://aka.ms/azuresentinel) | [Learn more about Solutions](https://aka.ms/azuresentinelsolutionsdoc)

options.text: This Solution installs the data connector for StealthTalk. You can get StealthTalk custom log data in your Microsoft Sentinel workspace. After installing the solution, configure and enable this data connector by following guidance in Manage solution view.

link.label: Learn more about connecting data sources

options.text: This Microsoft Sentinel Solution installs workbooks. Workbooks provide a flexible canvas for data monitoring, analysis, and the creation of rich visual reports within the Azure portal. They allow you to tap into one or many data sources from Microsoft Sentinel and combine them into unified interactive experiences.

Real-time SOC dashboard for the four classes of anomalous authentication events forwarded by StealthTalk into Microsoft Sentinel. 17 panels across Overview, Off-Hours, New Devices, Geo Anomaly and Brute Force sections.
This Microsoft Sentinel Solution installs scheduled analytic rules for StealthTalk anomalous-auth detections. These rules can be configured upon installation.

Detects systematic off-hours activity for a single StealthTalk user â€” repeated authentications
outside the user's configured working hours OR on weekends, observed across at least two
distinct calendar days within a 48-hour window. The pattern is a common indicator of credential
misuse, insider threat, or compromise of the account by an attacker operating in a different
timezone.

An "off-hours event" is one where IsWeekend=true OR DeviationMinutes >= 180 (i.e. >= 3 hours
after the configured working-hours end). Three or more such events on at least two distinct
days are required for an incident to fire â€” a single late-evening login is not enough.

Detects a single StealthTalk user registering two or more previously-unseen devices within
a 24-hour window. Sudden registration of multiple new devices is a strong indicator of
account takeover (an attacker enrolling their own device after credential theft) or
device-farm abuse.

The rule fires when a user produces two or more distinct NewDeviceId values within the
lookback window, regardless of operating system. Look at the OSList custom detail to spot
cross-platform patterns (e.g. an iOS-only user suddenly enrolling an Android device).

Detects a single StealthTalk authentication originating from a country or city that does
not match the user's assigned (expected) geographic zone. Each individual mismatch is
treated as an incident â€” there is no aggregation threshold, since a single login from an
unexpected country is high-confidence evidence of a credential issue.

An incident fires when LoginCountry differs from AssignedCountry OR LoginCity differs
from AssignedCity. Source IPv4, raw event ID, and both the observed and expected
geo-locations are surfaced as entities and custom details for the SOC analyst.

Detects a brute-force authentication attempt against a single StealthTalk user account.
The StealthTalk anti-brute-force subsystem emits a `MultiFailLogin` event when consecutive
failed authentication attempts from a user trigger an automatic account lockout. This
rule fires when the reported number of consecutive failures is 9 or more, indicating a
determined credential-guessing attack rather than a legitimate user fat-fingering.

The lockout duration (LoginBlockingSeconds) is surfaced as a custom detail so the SOC
analyst can prioritise based on attack persistence.
This Microsoft Sentinel Solution installs hunting queries for StealthTalk that the SOC team can leverage during proactive threat-hunting activities.

Detects a single StealthTalk user authenticating from two different countries within a
30-minute window â€” a strong indicator of a stolen credential, given that legitimate physical
travel between countries cannot occur on that timescale. Returns paired GeoAnomalyLogin
events ordered by elapsed time (smallest gap first), which makes it easy to spot the most
egregious cases at the top of the result set. This hunting query depends on StealthTalkAnomalousAuth data connector (StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL Parser or Table)

Detects the classic three-stage account-takeover pattern within a single 24-hour window
for the same StealthTalk user: an off-hours login (initial credential test), a new device
registration (attacker enrolling their own client), and a geo-anomaly login (attacker
authenticating from a different region). Any single one of these events is suspicious;
all three for the same account inside a day is high-confidence evidence of compromise. This hunting query depends on StealthTalkAnomalousAuth data connector (StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery3
elements.label: StealthTalk - Brute Force Followed By Suspicious Access

options.text:
Detects credential-stuffing or brute-force success: a `MultiFailLogin` event with 9 or more
failed attempts that is followed (within 2 hours) by either a `GeoAnomalyLogin` (login from
unusual location) or a `NewDeviceLogin` (login from new device) for the same user. Because
StealthTalk does not emit a "successful login" event in its anomalous-auth feed, the
presence of a subsequent anomaly event is the closest proxy for "the brute-force eventually
succeeded". This hunting query depends on StealthTalkAnomalousAuth data connector (StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL Parser or Table)
This Microsoft Sentinel Solution installs playbooks for StealthTalk to post anomalous-auth incidents to a Microsoft Teams channel.
